questions.question: Define Incremental Process Model and explain its basic principle.
keyPoints: Definition: A software development approach building product in small increments
Each increment delivers working version with additional features
Based on iterative development where feedback improves next increments
Allows changes in requirements between increments

questions.question: List and explain four advantages of Incremental Process Model.
keyPoints: Early delivery of working software - stakeholders can use partial system early
Flexibility to changes - requirements can be modified in subsequent increments
Risk reduction - each increment is tested, problems found early
Better user feedback - users can evaluate and provide feedback after each increment
Easy to test and debug - smaller increments are easier to test

questions.question: Explain the four phases of Incremental Process Model with a diagram.
keyPoints: Requirements: Gather initial requirements and prioritize
Design and Development: Build each increment with specified features
Deployment: Deliver completed increment to customers
Maintenance: Fix issues and prepare for next increment
Diagram showing cyclic nature of these phases

questions.question: Differentiate between Iterative and Incremental development approaches.
keyPoints: Iterative: Focuses on refining and improving existing features through repetition
Incremental: Focuses on adding new functionality in each cycle
Iterative improves quality of same components
Incremental builds new components on existing ones
Both can be used together in modern methodologies

questions.question: What is the RAD model? Explain its key characteristics.
keyPoints: RAD stands for Rapid Application Development
Emphasizes short development cycles (60-90 days)
Uses component-based construction
Heavy reliance on prototyping
Encourages user participation and feedback
Uses reusable components to speed development

questions.question: Explain when Incremental Process Model is most suitable with appropriate examples.
keyPoints: Suitable when: Requirements not fully known upfront
When requirements may change during development
When early delivery of partial system is needed
For long-term projects with evolving needs
Examples: E-commerce platforms, Enterprise software, Mobile apps with feature updates

questions.question: Explain the concept of MVP (Minimum Viable Product) in Incremental development.
keyPoints: MVP is the smallest version of product that can be released
Contains only core features necessary for early adopters
Allows validation of product idea with minimal investment
Feedback from MVP guides future development
Reduces risk by testing market before full investment

questions.question: Explain Incremental Process Model in detail. Discuss its phases, advantages, disadvantages, and when it should be used with examples.
keyPoints: Definition and basic concept
Four phases: Requirements, Design & Development, Deployment, Maintenance
Detailed explanation of each phase
Minimum 4 advantages with explanation
Minimum 3 disadvantages with explanation
When to use (suitable conditions)
Real-world examples where applicable
Comparison with other models

questions.question: Compare Incremental Process Model with Waterfall Model. Discuss why Incremental model is preferred in modern software development.
keyPoints: Comparison table of key aspects
Waterfall: Sequential, requirements upfront, less flexible
Incremental: Iterative, flexible requirements, early delivery
Why modern development prefers incremental:
- Better risk management
- Early user feedback
- Faster time-to-market
Situations where Waterfall may still be preferred
Conclusion on model selection

questions.question: What is the 'build-fix' problem in Incremental Model? How can it be avoided? Explain with suitable strategies.
keyPoints: Definition of build-fix problem
Causes: Poor initial planning, inadequate design, rushing to build
Consequences: Repeated fixes, design issues in future increments
Strategies to avoid:
- Proper initial design and architecture
- Thorough planning before each increment
- Code reviews and testing
- Documentation of decisions
- Regular feedback integration
Best practices for successful incremental development

questions.question: Explain the concept of prototyping in Incremental Process Models. How does it help in software development? Discuss different types of prototypes.
keyPoints: Definition of prototyping in incremental context
Purpose: Validate requirements early
Types of prototypes:
- Throwaway/Rapid prototype: Built quickly, discarded
- Evolutionary prototype: Built to evolve into final product
- Incremental prototype: Combine increments into final system
Benefits: Early user feedback, reduced ambiguity, better communication
Risks if not managed properly
Best practices for effective prototyping
